ogTitle: Cabramatta West vs Caddens: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Cabramatta West and Caddens.
heading: Cabramatta West vs Caddens — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Cabramatta West, New South Wales and Caddens, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Cabramatta West has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Caddens (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Cabramatta West or Caddens safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Cabramatta West has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Caddens scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Cabramatta West scores 42/100 for bushfire risk. Caddens scores 22/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Cabramatta West or Caddens?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Cabramatta West has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
